name: Band-Resisted Neck Flexion
resistance_profile: Band tension rises as the head flexes forward, so it is hardest near chin-to-chest (peak contraction) and easiest at the stretch.
cues: Move only the head and keep the torso still
Slow, smooth tempo with no jerking
execution_steps: Anchor a light band at about head height behind you, or loop it and hold the free end behind your head.
Set the band across the back of your head and take a tall, braced stance facing away from the anchor.
Let the band gently draw your head into slight extension to start.
Flex your neck, tucking your chin toward your chest against the band.
Pause briefly at the top, then return slowly to the start under control.
good_when: You want joint-friendly, easily scalable neck flexion without balancing a plate on your face
You are a beginner or a contact-sport athlete adding neck volume with minimal equipment
bad_when: You need heavy overload, since band tension is limited and lightest at the stretch
The band tends to slip off the head or dig into the face
common_errors: Using the whole trunk to yank the head down
Snapping through reps instead of controlling them
progressions: Step further from the anchor to increase band tension
Use a thicker band or add reps
